name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I'm proud to endorse these Democrats running up and down the ballot
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
In 2020, Democrats can win big up and down the ballot and all across the country. So today, I'm proud to endorse a new group of candidates across the country who are fighting to root out corruption... | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28525778/45c30650/1c95e22b4/66e9edc4/2524308928/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
In 2020, Democrats can win big up and down the ballot and all across the country.

That's the only way we'll be able to make the bold reforms we need in 2021 and beyond.

And it's only going to happen if we all get in the fight.

Today, I'm proud to endorse a new group of candidates across the country who are fighting to root out corruption and put power in the hands of the people, where it belongs:

**State Legislature**

Iman Jodeh (Colorado HD-41st)

Kayla Koether (Iowa HD-55th)

Roslyn Ogburn (Michigan HD-9th)

Patrick Burke (New York HD-142st)

Laurie Pohutsky (Michigan HD-19th)

Cindy Polo (Florida HD-103rd)

Rui Xu (Kansas HD-25th)

**District Attorney**

George Gascon (Los Angeles County)

**Secretary of State**

Shemia Fagan (Oregon)

**U.S. House of Representatives**

Jahana Hayes (Connecticut 5th)

Julie Oliver (Texas 25th)

Kiani Gardner (Alabama 1st)

Joyce Elliott (Arkansas 2nd)

Patricia Timmons-Goodson (North Carolina 8th)

Pam Keith (Florida 18th)

Mike Siegel (Texas 10th)

Susan Wild (Pennsylvania 7th)

**U.S. Senate**

John Hickenlooper (Colorado)

Mike Espy (Mississippi)

**[We can elect these candidates and more-- but only if we fight side by side as a grassroots movement. If you're in a place where you're able to make a donation, please chip in $2 or whatever you can today to help elect Warren Democrats like these all over America.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28525778/45c30650/1c95e22b4/66e9edc7/2524308928/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LXdkLWp1bjIwZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNUzEzRl9BTk5fRU5EX0VfMjAyMDA2MjBfVjFTMl9QIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-wd-jun20fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMS13F_ANN_END_E_20200620_V1S2_P")**

It's powerfully important that we win the White House this November, but we can't stop there.

Unless we keep the House, take back the Senate, and pick up seats in state legislatures across the nation, we'll just keep running into the buzzsaw of Republican obstruction.

And to help protect our elections, ensure voting rights, rethink our approach to public safety, and end mass incarceration, we need to elect the right people as secretaries of state and local prosecutors.

So as Warren Democrats, we're scouring the country to find candidates who are ready to make big, structural change at every level of government -- and that's exactly why I've endorsed these strong Democrats today.

Side by side, we'll lay out a bold vision for our future, fight for it, and win. We'll lift up the voices of people who've been -- at best -- ignored by a corrupt system, and -- at worst -- kicked in the teeth by a country that only works for a thin slice at the top. And we'll make our democracy work for everyone, not just the rich and powerful.

**[When we organize together, fight together, and persist together, we can win together. If you can, please make a contribution to support Democrats up and down the ticket and our re-election campaign today.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28525778/45c30650/1c95e22b4/66e9edc7/2524308928/VEsEAw/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LXdkLWp1bjIwZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNUzEzRl9BTk5fRU5EX0VfMjAyMDA2MjBfVjFTMl9QIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-wd-jun20fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMS13F_ANN_END_E_20200620_V1S2_P")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

Friend, _**Did you see our post this morning?**_ Elise Stefanik took to social media and voiced her support for our campaign because she knows **I am ready to help her fight in Congress alongside President Trump to accomplish his America First Agenda!** Watch her video here: [](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/mEvesZppy7639ZbJ0GbDAClA/t6w47sIt1FDHL2YTbbPnkA) As Rep. Stefanik said - _**" Every single vote counts. We need to hold this seat and ensure our next representative from New York's 27th district is a Republican who is a strong supporter of President Trump!"**_ With _**just 3 days until the critical elections on June 23rd**_ , it is crucially important that you make a plan to vote and have your voice heard - every single vote matters! [Find your polling place here.](http://sendy.sentbybeast.com/l/OAst8O6Rl3q4KZFnr763R83g/PF5t763NkgsIQJrFSV9892pqqQ/t6w47sIt1FDHL2YTbbPnkA) We need strong, conservative, and experienced leadership in Congress, and as Elise Stefanik said, _**" It is so important to get out and vote in support of Chris Jacobs!"**_ On June 23rd we can send a clear message to Nancy Pelosi and her liberal cohorts that their time in power is coming to an end and a new majority will take over Congress in November. _**You must get out and vote in both elections on June 23rd, and together we can ensure Western New York has the conservative leadership it deserves!**_ Thank you for your continued support. Sincerely,
Chris Jacobs

name: Team Eugene
subject: Help wish Eugene a happy Father’s Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DePasquale for PA 10

name: Sully Bunkeddeko
subject: Adem makes me proud to be a father
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/r-OAY_yexA7KNxCkIW0bfpPvnE7kZ-W4sE60_7H6tE8=)

Peter,

In our family, we don't normally spend Father's Day calling all of our friends and neighbors and telling them to go vote -- but when your son is just days away from becoming a Democratic nominee for Congress, fatherhood takes on a whole new meaning.

I've never had the words to describe just how proud I am of Adem -- of the sacrifices he's made to help others, his devotion to his community, and his passion for improving the lives of everyday families like ours. But I can tell you this: **[There's no one more deserving or more capable of representing working people in Washington than my son.](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/iHgqHCiG-NlLHoglGmJf5OtAm0jkLxtLVcR8UwXW3Qo=)**

When my wife, Jean, and I immigrated to the United States from Uganda, we dreamed of a future for our kids that was bright and full of promise. But we never could have imagined that, one day, our son would have the opportunity to fight for that very same future for all families. It's always been Adem's mission in life to make a difference, and he needs our help today so that he can make all of Brooklyn as proud as he's made me.

Just like I can't think of a better way to spend Father's Day than by getting out the vote for my son, I can't think of a better Father's Day gift than to see him get elected on Tuesday. And right now, he's counting on all of us to help make that happen. **[So join me today, and let's send my son to Washington.](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/iHgqHCiG-NlLHoglGmJf5OtAm0jkLxtLVcR8UwXW3Qo=)**

******[HELP ADEM WIN](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/P6AECw3YgDwmFW0slj-Ld6knU28jKyHR4ADcH_Eill8=)******
Thank you,
Sully Bunkeddeko

* * *

******[DONATE TODAY](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/iHgqHCiG-NlLHoglGmJf5OtAm0jkLxtLVcR8UwXW3Qo=)******

******[BECOME A VOLUNTEER](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/eYu6fniQIIoZMXv8uEJbBt1PmRmn7_RDtv9d3SNU5Mw=)******

******[VISIT OUR WEBSITE](https://act.myngp.com/el/4FeUpfrkYQMl669c7BWpeLC4JcknohSo-eLEIWP5gHg=/3Vity8qEt7_hKGR_EL9oeqa3bdaOinWfoJr6THhVOOY=)******

name: MJ Hegar
subject: The dads in my life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Thank you to all the great dads out there.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901a9/7f51f7bc/4ea7a827/4ef6db78/700761621/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | Hi Peter,

A few years after my mom left my biological father after years of abuse, she married my stepfather. He loved me as his own, and showed me that it takes more than just having kids to be a dad. He taught me that the warrior spirit wasn't just for men, and to rise to a worthwhile challenge no matter how hard the fight might be.

My dad always had my back, until he was killed in a workplace accident when I was in college. His death is one of the many reasons I'm committed to fighting for workers' rights. Because of the safety standards won by the labor movement, fewer families have to go through the devastation mine did.

The other incredible father in my life is my husband, Brandon, who's been by my side throughout this whole campaign. Running a Senate campaign in a state as big as Texas with a five-year-old and three-year-old isn't easy, and it wouldn't be possible without Brandon. Whether on the road with me and the boys, making sure they didn't get into too much mischief while I was campaigning, or taking care of them at home while still working at his full-time job -- I couldn't ask for a more loving father for my boys.

Together, with all of you, we're fighting for a brighter future for our kids -- a planet that's habitable, a society without the constant threat of gun violence, and a just world where the zip code you're born in doesn't determine the trajectory of your life.

Today, for Fathers' Day, I wanted to say thank you to all of those fathers teaching their kids the value of hard work, grit, and fighting for what's right -- and who are out there fighting for a better world for all our kids. Happy Fathers' Day,

MJ

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/648901a9/7f51f7bc/4ea7a827/4ef6db7d/700761621/VEsC/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: We appreciate fathers!
body:
_“It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men.”_ **– Frederick Douglass** _“A good father is one of the most unsung, unpraised, unnoticed, and yet one of the most valuable assets in our society.”_ **– Billy Graham** Happy Father’s Day to all of the dads and grandpas out there! The quotes above perfectly encapsulate how important fathers are – not just to individuals and families, but also to our nation and world. How many problems that we face today stem from fatherlessness and the children who have grown up broken, without strong role models? How many people in our nation do not see how valuable an asset fathers our to our entire society? We love dads; we recognize your importance and we thank you for all you do for your children, grandchildren, families, communities, and country. And, we know that for different reasons, Father’s Day can be a difficult day for some of you, and we send our love and prayers to you. Have a wonderful Father’s Day, and enjoy your family and your blessings in the midst of the trials our nation faces. In liberty, Jenny Beth Martin & the support team | |

clean_body: “It is easier to build strong children than to repair broken men.” – Frederick Douglass “A good father is one of the most unsung, unpraised, unnoticed, and yet one of the most valuable assets in our society.” – Billy Graham Happy Father’s Day to all of the dads and grandpas out there! The quotes above perfectly encapsulate how important fathers are – not just to individuals and families, but also to our nation and world. How many problems that we face today stem from fatherlessness and the children who have grown up broken, without strong role models? How many people in our nation do not see how valuable an asset fathers our to our entire society? We love dads; we recognize your importance and we thank you for all you do for your children, grandchildren, families, communities, and country. And, we know that for different reasons, Father’s Day can be a difficult day for some of you, and we send our love and prayers to you. Have a wonderful Father’s Day, and enjoy your family and your blessings in the midst of the trials our nation faces. name: Rita Hart
subject: Help me celebrate Paul
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/vG6jEwVKYpYEIm8rNiBsrxWdEMudP2xAh9MQHteH2r8=/GRgLbH52IS5iMab08CKomi3MAm05WO_Yc98D0PQC5CU=?refcode=200621-email) Happy Father's Day to the incredible men who have taken on one of the most challenging but rewarding experiences in life - being a father. I hope we can all take today to acknowledge the men in our lives who have been positive father figures. I'd like to take some time to talk about a man near and dear to me, my husband and the father of my children, Paul Hart. Paul and I have been married for almost 38 years and during that time, I have known him as a man who is always there for his friends and family. He has shown incredible loyalty in our marriage and commitment in raising our five children and continues to grow in his role as father and grandfather. Paul really loves his children and his grandchildren. I know that today he is looking forward to spending some virtual time with them. Like many of you, it's been hard for Paul to be apart from them during this pandemic. "Pops," as the grandkids call him, lives to make those four kiddos smile and laugh and watching him do that brings such joy to our lives. Paul is someone who brings the fun. He certainly knows how to tease me and makes me laugh when I really need a lift. He joins us on the road (when he can) and engages with constituents and campaign staff we meet along the way. Paul is especially known for giving staff the opportunity to drive a tractor when they need a break. I continue to learn from Paul every day. His common sense and his realistic way of looking at the world keep me focused on the things that matter in this campaign. Thank you for loving me, Paul, thank you for loving our children and grandchildren and thank you for giving me all the time and support you give as I work to represent this district. You are my biggest supporter and my very best friend. Happy Father's Day, Paul. Thank you for everything you do! \--Rita Hart | ****[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.myngp.com/el/vG6jEwVKYpYEIm8rNiBsrxWdEMudP2xAh9MQHteH2r8=/GRgLbH52IS5iMab08CKomi3MAm05WO_Yc98D0PQC5CU=?refcode=200621-email)****

subject: I was pepper-sprayed.
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by the Congressional Black Caucus PAC
body:
|
---
| [](https://go.cbcpac.%3D/?g=iRqZOt8pCaBe6wkNseiFmTA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ms_cbc_fr_q22020_cbcpac-beatty-split?refcode=MS_EM_FR_2020.06.21_B4_Joyce-Beatty-Personal_X__F1_S1_C1__all_RS&amount=5amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000")

[On Saturday May 30, 2020, I was pepper sprayed in downtown Columbus while trying to de-escalate a tense situation between protestors and police. We were all there to protest the deaths of Ahmaud Arbery, Breonna Taylor, and George Floyd.](https://go.cbcpac.%3D/?g=iRqZOt8pCaBe6wkNseiFmTA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ms_cbc_fr_q22020_cbcpac-beatty-split?refcode=MS_EM_FR_2020.06.21_B4_Joyce-Beatty-Personal_X__F1_S1_C1__all_RS&amount=5amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000")

This has never happened to me before.

As a little girl in Ohio during the 1960s, I remember drinking from a different water fountain and swimming in a different public pool from White folks.

But never in my life have I ever faced this kind of injustice, even after participating in countless civil rights demonstrations.

Although I'm outraged that this happened, **I refuse to be desensitized.** I know I'm not the first, and I won't be the last.

Every American, regardless of color, deserves the right to live freely and make their voice heard without violent backlash and retaliation.

That's why as a Congresswoman representing Ohio's great 3rd District, I refuse to be silent. [As long as racism is alive and well in this country, I refuse to give up on the fight for equality and social justice. If you're with me, will you donate $5 today to the Congressional Black Caucus PAC to continue our fight?](https://go.cbcpac.%3D/?g=iRqZOt8pCaBe6wkNseiFmTA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ms_cbc_fr_q22020_cbcpac-beatty-split?refcode=MS_EM_FR_2020.06.21_B4_Joyce-Beatty-Personal_X__F1_S1_C1__all_RS&amount=5amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000") | | [$5 ➞](https://go.cbcpac.%3D/?g=iRqZOt8pCaBe6wkNseiFmTA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ms_cbc_fr_q22020_cbcpac-beatty-split?refcode=MS_EM_FR_2020.06.21_B4_Joyce-Beatty-Personal_X__F1_S1_C1__all_RS&amount=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000")

The Congressional Black Caucus works to increase the number of African Americans in the U.S. Congress, support non-Black candidates that champion our interests, and promote African American participation in the political process.

We're committed to getting African Americans and non-Black allies elected to the highest positions in Congress and activated at the polls to advance issues that matter in our communities.

By joining this grassroots movement, you are helping uplift African Americans in the political process. Our campaign is powered by supporters like you. clean_body: | | "" "" This has never happened to me before. As a little girl in Ohio during the 1960s, I remember drinking from a different water fountain and swimming in a different public pool from White folks. But never in my life have I ever faced this kind of injustice, even after participating in countless civil rights demonstrations. Although I'm outraged that this happened, I refuse to be desensitized. I know I'm not the first, and I won't be the last. Every American, regardless of color, deserves the right to live freely and make their voice heard without violent backlash and retaliation. That's why as a Congresswoman representing Ohio's great 3rd District, I refuse to be silent. "" | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" I firmly believe we all must be proactive in this movement to demand accountability from those who took an oath to protect and serve. That's why I'm asking you to do your part today.
